$comment: Each vector encodes the manifest per §3.2 as
and hashes the resulting ASCII bytes with sha256. `commitment` is the
lowercase hex digest. `input` is the exact pre-hash string for debug.
publicKeyHex carries the raw bytes; tests decode it and compare against
computeManifestCommitment(). If this file drifts from the encoder,

vectors.name: baseline-zero-key
vectors.description: Fixed (backendId, algorithmSuite, publicKey) triple producing a known digest. 32-byte zero key; base64 output is 44 chars with one '=' pad.

vectors.name: baseline-ff-key
vectors.description: Second baseline with an all-ones 32-byte key — guards against accidental zero-key-specific shortcuts in the encoder.

vectors.name: cross-suite-distinguishability
vectors.description: Same backendId + publicKey as baseline-zero-key, different algorithmSuite. Commitment MUST differ from baseline-zero-key (§3.3 case 2).

vectors.name: cross-backend-distinguishability
vectors.description: Same algorithmSuite + publicKey as baseline-zero-key, different backendId. Commitment MUST differ from baseline-zero-key (§3.3 case 3).

vectors.name: base64-padding-residue-1
vectors.description: publicKey length = 1, base64 output has two '=' pads (length mod 3 == 1). §3.3 case 4.

vectors.name: base64-padding-residue-2
vectors.description: publicKey length = 2, base64 output has one '=' pad (length mod 3 == 2). §3.3 case 4.

vectors.name: base64-padding-residue-0
vectors.description: publicKey length = 33, base64 output has no '=' pad (length mod 3 == 0). Completes §3.3 case 4 by covering all three residue classes.
